questions.question: Which of the following numbers belongs to the set of Natural Numbers (ℕ) under its standard pure mathematical definition?
questions.explanation: Standard natural numbers represent the positive counting integers {1, 2, 3, ...}. Negative integers, fractions, and zero are excluded from this standard set definition.

questions.question: The set of Natural Numbers is closed under which of the following pairs of arithmetic operations?
questions.explanation: Adding or multiplying any two natural numbers always yields another natural number. This does not hold for subtraction (e.g., 2 - 5 = -3) or division (e.g., 1 / 2 = 0.5).

questions.question: In the Peano Axioms, how is the successor of a natural number n formally defined?
questions.explanation: Peano axioms define the succession function S(n) as mapping a natural number to its next consecutive unit, which is algebraically equivalent to n + 1.
